summary: Phenomenal Owsley audience tape capturing a scorching Dark Star → Morning Dew suite; the audio is crystal‑clear for an AUD and the performance ranks among ’72’s finest.
review: This 1972 Owsley field recording delivers an almost pristine audience sound that many reviewers call “phenomenal” despite being an AUD, with only a minor unpatched cut in the Dark Star. The music shines – the Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence (the famed “stomp”) is repeatedly praised as one of the era’s greatest, eclipsing the lone dissent about it being “lazy.” Overall the show and its sound make this a must‑listen for serious Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance (exceptional Dark Star/Morning Dew) and audio quality (audience tape described as phenomenal), with only a single low‑rating outlier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Owsley's experimental mic placement yields a surprisingly clear, dynamic field recording; reviewers note it sounds “phenomenal” and “sweet,” with only one comment about an unpatched cut in the Dark Star.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A concise ~45‑minute segment that builds from Phil Lesh’s angular “stomp” into an expansive Dark Star, culminating in a searing Morning Dew; the flow is praised as tight and powerful.

band_member_comments.Phil: Several reviewers highlight Phil's opening ‘stomp’ bass riff as a standout moment that drives the suite.
